mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth is driven by demographic shifts rather than layoffs.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or quarterly returns are negative despite concurrent demand expansion in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side stress and reduced factory utilization, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered observations, or rolling IC remains below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and increases default risk, leading to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is below 2%, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward tests over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon as market participants price in improved economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly payrolls contractions indicate severe labor market deterioration, which rapidly reduces consumer confidence and spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during expansionary periods, or forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered cases during non-recessionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite inflation spikes, or 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances across a 3-year evaluation period.
